headline: Tuvalu FIRST BASELINE CONFIRM at 48.4 / Functional — April 14 state of emergency on Funafuti over fuel and electricity supply (worsening Iran-war-linked fuel crisis with 40% diesel and 30% petrol price spikes; two-week emergency; outages up to 20 hours) is an active governance-capacity signal. State of emergency declared by Head of State to give government broad powers to control fuel/light/transport consumption — appropriate institutional response to acute resource crisis. Fuel supply 'not assured beyond June' per Transport Minister Kofe. Energy stable as of April 27 with backup-generator procurement underway. Structural context: UNGA 2025 Advisory Opinion confirmed Tuvalu retains sovereignty even if territorially submerged (BND structural positive); 90%+ of Tuvaluans applied under Australia 2025 visa scheme; 95% projected underwater at high tides by 2100; Tuvalu to host special world leaders meeting prior to UNFCCC 2026 in Turkey — active climate advocacy posture. Net assessment: emergency declaration is a competent governance response under existential climate-vulnerability conditions; no negative regression; published 48.4 sustained.
key_evidence: April 14, 2026 (recent in-cycle anchor): Tuvalu's Head of State declared State of Emergency on Funafuti for two weeks — increasing instability in electricity generation/distribution + credible fuel supply risks. Funafuti houses ~75% of Tuvalu's 10,000 population.
April 1, 2026: Diesel prices up 40%, petrol up 30% in Tuvalu — Iran-war-linked supply shock cascading to Pacific microstate.
April 27, 2026 (PINA): Energy stable but risks remain per Minister Kofe; backup generator procurement underway.
RNZ: Tuvalu fuel supply 'not assured beyond June' per Transport Minister Kofe.
UNGA 2025 Advisory Opinion: Tuvalu and atoll states retain sovereignty and statehood even if territory submerged — BND/EQU structural positive for rights of sinking-island populations.
Australia 2025 visa scheme: 90%+ of Tuvaluans applied for residency/citizenship — climate-migration governance signal.
Tuvalu to host special world leaders meeting prior to UNFCCC 2026 in Turkey — active climate advocacy posture.
Tuvalu 95% projected underwater at high tides by 2100 — structural existential climate risk.

evidence.source: RNZ — Tuvalu declares state of emergency over fuel and power supply concerns
evidence.finding: State of emergency declared April 14; two weeks; Funafuti scope; government powers to control supplies.

evidence.source: PINA — Tuvalu declares State of Emergency over power fuel risks
evidence.finding: Multi-source Pacific media confirmation of emergency declaration; structural governance response.

evidence.source: Bloomberg — Tuvalu Declares State of Emergency on Worsening Fuel Crisis Linked to Iran War
evidence.finding: Iran-war supply-shock cascading to Pacific microstate; 40% diesel, 30% petrol increases April 1; international context.

evidence.source: RNZ — Tuvalu fuel supply not assured beyond June
evidence.finding: Minister Kofe public statement on forward fuel-supply uncertainty; transparent ACC/AWR posture.

evidence.source: PINA — Tuvalu energy stable but risks remain as Minister Kofe outlines backup plan after emergency
evidence.finding: Post-emergency stabilization; backup generator procurement; sustained operational response.

evidence.source: UN News — UNGA Advisory Opinion on statehood for atoll states
evidence.finding: Sovereignty/statehood retained even if territorially submerged — BND/EQU structural positive for sinking-island populations.

evidence.source: Lowy Institute — Lost at sea: How Tuvalu's plight holds lessons
evidence.finding: Structural context for Tuvalu's climate-vulnerability governance challenges and migration policy responses.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.0 sustained at published. A1 detection: Minister-level public disclosure of forward fuel-supply uncertainty; A4 amplification: international media engagement on climate-vulnerability narrative. Anchor 3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.0 sustained at published. E1 affective resonance: state-level concern for Funafuti population's acute resource access; E2 protected scope: emergency-powers framework prioritizes population essential services. Anchor 3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.0 sustained at published. AC1 responsiveness: 24-hour emergency declaration in response to supply-shock; AC4 resource mobilization: backup-generator procurement initiated; emergency-powers framework operationalized. Anchor 3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.5 sustained at published. EQ1 universality: emergency targets Funafuti (75% population); outer-island resource access patterns less documented. Conservative hold at 2.5.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.0 sustained at published. B3 scope clarity: active climate-advocacy international engagement; B5 multilateral engagement: UNGA Advisory Opinion (sovereignty), Australia visa scheme, UNFCCC pre-summit hosting role. Anchor 3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.0 sustained at published. AB1 harm-acknowledgment: Minister Kofe publicly disclosed fuel-supply uncertainty; AB2 transparency: emergency declaration with defined two-week duration and scope. Anchor 3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.0 sustained at published. S2 long-term-impact: backup-generator procurement creates structural redundancy; S5 coalitional compassion: international advocacy on atoll-state rights. Anchor 3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.0 sustained at published. I1 consistency: declared climate-vulnerability concern + state-emergency operational response = values-action alignment under acute pressure; I3 follow-through: post-emergency operational continuity (April 27 stabilization). Anchor 3 sustained.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstructs cleanly to 48.4 = published. Sub-threshold drift (0.0). Functional band sustained. Not a boundary case.

negativeSignals: Fuel supply 'not assured beyond June' — forward-looking vulnerability persists; outer-island access patterns less documented (conservative EQU hold); 95% projected underwater at high tides by 2100 (existential climate context); 90%+ Tuvaluans applied for Australian visa scheme — exit-as-adaptation pattern signals long-term population displacement trajectory.
positiveSignals: State of emergency declared as appropriate institutional response to acute crisis; backup-generator procurement under way; post-emergency stabilization (April 27); UNGA 2025 Advisory Opinion on atoll-state sovereignty; active climate advocacy (UNFCCC pre-summit hosting); transparent Minister-level public disclosure; coordination with Australia on climate-migration governance.
sector_context: Pacific Island States cluster. Tuvalu's 48.4 (Functional) baseline is structurally robust under climate-existential conditions: the state demonstrates competent governance response capacity despite microstate constraints. Cross-reference: Kiribati (similar climate-vulnerability cluster), Marshall Islands (similar Compact/migration cluster), Fiji and Micronesia (Pacific regional context).
open_actions_resolved: First baseline established with state-of-emergency event as recent governance-capacity anchor
UNGA Advisory Opinion documented as BND/EQU structural positive
Australia visa scheme uptake documented as climate-migration signal
open_actions_carried: Watch: June fuel-supply continuity (Minister Kofe forward signal)
Watch: UNFCCC 2026 pre-summit hosting role and outcomes
Watch: post-Iran-war global fuel market stabilization for Pacific resupply
